title: The Verdant Maw
atAGlance: The Verdant Maw is a drowned jungle basin where strangler-fig cathedrals, sinkwater causeways, and vine-bridged ruin towers choke out horizon and map alike. Most travelers move by named cuts rather than true roads: Lantern Scar (a burn-marked ridge route), Choir Steps (old cyclopean stairs drowned to the knee), and Broker's Span (a ropeway rebuilt every season by Concord crews). The Maw is rich in resin, relic stone, and medicinal spores, yet wealth survives only behind disciplined camp routines and armed watchfires. Storm fog rolls in by dusk, apex predators shadow caravan noise for miles, and a single wrong halt can turn a supply run into a disappearance.
sageNotes: The Verdant Maw rewards memory and punishes arrogance. Veterans still navigate by old anchors such as Blackroot Ferry, the lightning-cut Glasswater Obelisk, and the Red Bell Grove where bonewood gongs warn of migration swarms too large to fight. The Rootbound Choir guards sacred paths behind seed-oaths and ritual tolls, while Cinder Concord surveyors burn lane marks into bark and sell route guarantees that weather and hunger often void. Those who live by the basin's rhythm know names as well as landmarks: Sister Amri Mossvein, who reads canopy shifts like storm charts, and Quartermaster Dorn Kettlehook, who swaps dry rations for relic claims and silence. In the Maw, every travel choice buys one safety and spends another.
gameInformation: Use a Travel Leg procedure for Maw crossings: declare destination and route (Lantern Scar, Choir Steps, or Broker's Span), then assign roles (Lead Scout, Rear Watch, Forager, Load Keeper). At each leg, run one Terrain Test and one Threat Test; on failure apply one pressure result instead of full stop (1 exhaustion spread, 1d4 supply loss, delayed arrival, or apex trail marker added). Maw Pressure Clock beats: 2 (leech-rain and sickness checks), 4 (predator shadowing and night harassment), 6 (camp breach or flash-flood split party), 8 (full route collapse and forced detour into ruin hex). Reusable complications: false Concord burn-marks, Rootbound ward totems that trigger spirit hostility if disturbed, drowned vault vents releasing toxic spores, and refugee columns consuming local forage. Reward smart preparation with tangible gains: hidden cache maps, temporary safe-camp status, reduced clock ticks, and leverage with Rootbound or Concord contacts for later regional favors.
